summary: Closed, unmerged PR adding a decompilation of fighter collision-related function func_8007B7A4 in ftcoll.c plus its ftcoll.h prototype. The function gets the Fighter from a fighter GObj-like argument, updates fp->x1994 if arg1 is larger, sets fp->x198C to 2 when fp->x1990 is nonzero or 1 otherwise, then calls func_800BFFD0(fp, 9, 0). Review feedback did not address correctness; it requested waiting on PR #576 and consolidating future work into fewer PRs.
what_changed: Added a prototype for func_8007B7A4 to src/melee/ft/ftcoll.h.
The new function calls getFighter(fighter_gobj) to obtain Fighter* fp.
The new function compares arg1 against fp->x1994 and stores arg1 when it is greater.
The new function derives fp->x198C from fp->x1990: 2 if fp->x1990 is nonzero, otherwise 1.
The new function calls func_800BFFD0(fp, 9, 0) after updating fighter fields.

A decomp.me scratch link was included directly before the function as `https://decomp.me/scratch/wBYl7`, which in C tokenization effectively appears as an `https:` label followed by a line comment rather than a conventional comment.
smart_moves: Used an explicit temporary variable `var_r0` for the 1-or-2 assignment before writing fp->x198C, which may reflect an attempt to preserve compiler control flow/register behavior.
Used explicit `(s32)` casts around fp->x1994 and fp->x1990 in comparisons, suggesting attention to signed comparison behavior or field type ambiguity.
Kept the new implementation colocated with nearby ftcoll functions and exposed it through ftcoll.h.
decomp_lessons: When field types are not yet known, explicit casts can be useful for matching signedness-sensitive comparisons, but the resulting prototype and field usage should remain provisional.
A scratch URL should be put in a valid C comment form, e.g. `// https://...`, to avoid creating an unintended label such as `https:`.
Small single-function decomp PRs can be blocked by dependency/order concerns; reviewers may prefer batching by file or module instead of many tiny PRs.
Because this PR was closed unmerged and received no correctness review, the function body should be treated as a historical attempt rather than accepted project knowledge.
naming_conventions: Unknown Fighter fields were referenced with offset-style names `x198C`, `x1990`, and `x1994`.
Unidentified local and parameter names used generic decomp-style names such as `arg1` and `var_r0`.
The prototype used `void* fighter_gobj` rather than the more specific `HSD_GObj*` seen in nearby ftcoll.h prototypes, indicating type uncertainty in this PR.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Explicit signed casts: `if (arg1 > (s32) fp->x1994)` and `if ((s32) fp->x1990 != 0)`.
Branch-based assignment through `var_r0` instead of a direct ternary or boolean arithmetic.
Preserved the call sequence: update max-like field x1994, compute x198C from x1990, then call `func_800BFFD0(fp, 9, 0)`.
Included decomp.me scratch reference `wBYl7` as matching context, though not in a clean comment form.

review_feedback.body: Please wait on #576. In the future, you may want to submit fewer PRs with more commits each. Most people do one PR per file, or sometimes one PR per module (`ft`, `gx`, etc).

review_feedback: No line-level review comments or technical correctness feedback were present in the slice.
follow_up_queries: Check PR #576 to understand the dependency or sequencing issue that caused this PR to be paused.
Search later merged PRs for `func_8007B7A4` to see the accepted implementation, final prototype type, and any renamed fields.
Search for later names or documentation for Fighter fields `x198C`, `x1990`, and `x1994`.
Verify whether `func_800BFFD0(fp, 9, 0)` was later documented or renamed in fighter damage/collision state handling.
